title: phacelia neomexicana var. coulteri
intro: Phacelia neomexicana var. coulteri, also known as Coulteri's phacelia, is a variety of phacelia plant that belongs to the borage family (Boraginaceae) and is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat various health issues such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Phacelia neomexicana var. coulteri include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and act as an expectorant to relieve coughs. The plant can be prepared as an infusion, tincture, or salve, making it a versatile herbal remedy that can be used in various forms, such as a tea for digestive issues or a topical application for skin irritations.
